misconceptions.display_name: Smaller than a divergent series implies divergence
misconceptions.description: Concludes a_n diverges because a_n < b_n with b_n divergent; being below a divergent series says nothing.
trigger_phrases: it's smaller than a divergent series so it diverges
less than the harmonic series means it diverges

misconceptions.description: Bounds a_n below by a convergent series (a_n > convergent) and claims convergence; direct comparison for convergence needs a_n <= a convergent series.
trigger_phrases: a_n is bigger than a convergent series so it converges

misconceptions.display_name: Limit Comparison with c = 0 or infinity used carelessly
misconceptions.description: Applies the standard Limit Comparison conclusion when c = 0 or c = infinity, where it requires the special one-sided forms.
trigger_phrases: the ratio goes to zero so they behave the same
limit is infinite so both converge
